summary: Julia Evans recommends Bubblesort Zines by Amy Wibowo, a collection of computer science zines aimed at teenage girls that cover topics like sorting algorithms, networking, cryptography, and image processing. She highlights the 'How does the internet' zine for its impressive range of topics and the newly released 'Pixel Perfect' zine on image processing. Evans emphasizes that these zines explain complex concepts clearly without dumbing them down, making CS accessible to audiences who might not see themselves in traditional CS materials. She draws from her own experience of wanting to learn programming as a teenager but not knowing where to start.
key_points: Bubblesort Zines by Amy Wibowo fills a gap for CS education aimed at teenage girls, covering topics from sorting algorithms to cryptography to image processing
The zines explain complex concepts clearly without dumbing them down, respecting the intelligence of young readers
The mission is to make computer science accessible and inclusive to people who might not think there's a place for them in CS
Evans personally vouches for the quality, noting the 'How does the internet' zine covers an impressive range from carrier pigeons to TCP routing
money_quotes: Teenage girls are smart and curious people who are interested in learning complicated things about science & technology!
She explains all these things in a clear and fun way without dumbing down the ideas.
I definitely didn't understand the basics of how TCP worked when I was teenager learning HTML (or even when I started working as a professional web developer!)

Often material that's written for young people gets dumbed down or doesn't go into all the interesting details, but of course 13-14 year olds can understand a lot of complex concepts, as long as you explain them clearly!
Let's make sure that these tools & skills are accessible to everyone.
The goal of these zines is to show people that interesting computer science concepts like encryption, image processing, and computer networking can be for them.
key_insight: Complex computer science concepts don't need to be dumbed down for young or non-traditional audiences — they just need to be explained clearly and presented in an inclusive, welcoming way.
